clause_quote: This Base Vehicle Limited Warranty applies only to vehicles with an active base warranty mapping recorded against the VIN.
The base warranty covers factory-fitted vehicle components except items assigned to another warranty product or specifically excluded.
If the VIN cannot be matched to an active base warranty record, the claim is not eligible under this policy.
The Base Vehicle Limited Warranty provides coverage for 36 months from the in-service date or 100,000 km, whichever occurs first.
A claim remains within the time limit when the repair order date is on or before the recorded base warranty end date.
A claim remains within the mileage limit when the odometer reading is at or below the recorded base warranty mileage ceiling.
Covered base warranty components include factory-fitted steering, suspension, climate control, body electrical, and interior electronic systems unless otherwise excluded.
A claimed repair code must match the stated causal part and the covered component category for the claim to remain eligible.
A claim using a repair code for one component while naming a different causal part shall not be auto-approved.
Base warranty coverage applies only to failures caused by defects in material or workmanship.
A claim description must identify the customer complaint, the defect found, and the repair performed.
If the defect condition is not clearly described, the claim requires more information before adjudication.
Wear items are not covered for normal consumption and are eligible only when the dealer states a specific manufacturing defect.
A wear item claim must explain the defect condition that makes the failure different from ordinary service wear.
If the claim describes only wear to service limit or normal deterioration, the wear item claim shall be rejected.
The base warranty does not apply to failures caused by abuse, collision damage, unauthorized modification, or lack of maintenance.
If the failure description directly attributes damage to an excluded cause, the claim shall be rejected.
If the evidence of an exclusion is incomplete or disputed, the claim shall be referred for human review.
Every base warranty claim must include the VIN, in-service date, repair order date, odometer reading, repair code, causal part, and a specific failure description.
Electronic component claims require a diagnostic report that supports the claimed fault condition.
Claims with missing mandatory documentation shall not be approved and require completion before payment.
Labor reimbursement is evaluated against the published standard and maximum labor time for the repair code.
A claim within the published labor limit may proceed when all other coverage conditions are satisfied.
Labor time above the published maximum requires human review and cannot be auto-approved.
A base warranty claim shall be approved only when coverage, failure eligibility, documentation, and labor limits are all satisfied.
A base warranty claim shall be rejected when the vehicle is outside the warranty period, above the mileage limit, or subject to an exclusion.
A base warranty claim shall be referred when the evidence is conflicting, incomplete, or unusually ambiguous.
Goodwill assistance is discretionary and may be considered only when the claim is outside warranty solely because of time or mileage.
Goodwill requires a service history consistent with proper maintenance and a failure pattern that resembles a warrantable defect.
Goodwill cannot be auto-approved and must be referred for human authorization.
